learning.concept: Debugging is the process of finding and fixing mistakes in your program.
learning.explanation: If a program has a bug, it won't work correctly. To debug it, you have to find where the mistake is and then re-order or fix the steps. Debugging involves reading your code line-by-line to see where the logic breaks.

analogy.title: Finding a Glitch
analogy.body:
If you follow a recipe: 
1. Serve Tea
2. Boil Water
3. Add Powder
You would serve an empty cup first! You must re-order the steps to make it work. Debugging is re-ordering the logic.

practice.q: What is another name for a mistake in code?
When should you debug your code?
practice.a: As soon as you find something is wrong!

practice.q: Can you debug other people's code?
practice.a: Yes, if you understand the logic!
